---
title: "AERA Paper Overview"
format: html
editor: source
---

```{r setup}
#| include: false

library(tidyverse)
library(flextable)
library(pins)
library(ftExtra)
library(here)

if (interactive()) {
  params <- rmarkdown::yaml_front_matter(
    rstudioapi::getSourceEditorContext()$path
  )$params
}


percent_indicators <- c(
  "absenteeism",
  "graduation",
  "suspension",
  "college/career"
)

local_board <- board_folder(here("../cde_data_pipeline/data/pins"))

homeless <- pin_read(local_board, "homeless_enrollment_clean") |>
  filter(
    county_name == "Solano",
    charter_school == "All" | aggregate_level == "S",
    dass == "All" | aggregate_level == "S"
  ) |>
  mutate(
    percent_homeless = homeless_student_enrollment /
      cumulative_enrollment *
      100,
    across(ends_with("(percent)"), parse_number)
  )

solano_to_orange <- read_csv("data/solano_to_orange.csv") |>
  mutate(
    currstatus = if_else(
      indicator %in% percent_indicators,
      paste0(currstatus, "%"),
      as.character(currstatus)
    )
  )

homeless_lea <-
  homeless |>
  filter(
    (aggregate_level == "D" & charter_school == "All" & dass == "All") |
      (aggregate_level == "S" & charter_school == "Yes")
  )

homeless_count_cumulative <-
  homeless_lea |>
  filter(reporting_long == "Total Students") |>
  pull(homeless_student_enrollment) |>
  nth(1)

selected_to_orange <-
  solano_to_orange |>
  filter(
    color == 1,
    (aggregate_level == "S" & charter_school == "Yes") |
      (aggregate_level == "D")
  ) |>
  mutate(
    reporting_long = fct_relevel(
      as_factor(reporting_long),
      "Homeless Youth" # Foster Youth removed here
    )
  ) |>
  arrange(reporting_long)

homeless_count <-
  selected_to_orange |>
  # Swapped student_count for homeless_student_enrollment
  filter(reporting_long == "Homeless Youth", !is.na(homeless_student_enrollment))

```

```{r results_table}
#| echo: false
#| tbl-cap: "Number of indicator/student group pairs where the number of students needed to change the group to orange is less than the number of students from that group that are also in the homeless youth group."

districts <-
  selected_to_orange |>
  pull(district_name) |>
  unique()

district_count <- length(districts)

homeless_red <-
  selected_to_orange |>
  filter(reporting_long == "Homeless Youth", color == 1)

homeless_greater_than_change <-
  selected_to_orange |>
  filter(
    reporting_long != "Homeless Youth",
    color == 1,
    homeless_student_enrollment > 0
  ) |>
  mutate(greater = homeless_student_enrollment - students_to_orange) |>
  arrange(desc(greater)) |>
  select(
    academic_year,
    district_name,
    indicator,
    currnumer,
    currdenom,
    reporting_long,
    homeless_student_enrollment,
    students_to_orange
  )

student_group_counts <-
  homeless_greater_than_change |>
  group_by(academic_year, district_name) |>
  summarize("Indicator/Groups" = n())

# First, create the wide dataset with clean year columns
counts_wide <- student_group_counts |>
  mutate(
    district_name = case_match(
      district_name,
      "Benicia Unified" ~ "District B",
      "Dixon Unified" ~ "District A",
      "Fairfield-Suisun Unified" ~ "District D",
      "Travis Unified" ~ "District C",
      "Vacaville Unified" ~ "District F", 
      "Vallejo City Unified" ~ "District E"
    )
  ) |>
  arrange(district_name) |> 
  # Removed names_prefix so the columns are just the academic years
  pivot_wider(names_from = academic_year, values_from = "Indicator/Groups")

# Figure out exactly how many year columns exist so the header spans perfectly
num_year_columns <- ncol(counts_wide) - 1

# Build the flextable
counts_wide |>
  flextable() |>
  set_header_labels(district_name = "District") |>
  # Add the spanning top header
  add_header_row(
    values = c(
      "", # Empty space above "District"
      "Count of red indicator/student group combinations where s < h" # The long title
    ),
    colwidths = c(1, num_year_columns) # 1 column for District, rest for the title
  ) |>
  # Center align the new top header so it looks balanced over the years
  align(i = 1, part = "header", align = "center") |>
  # Add a horizontal line under the spanning header for visual separation
  hline(i = 1, j = 2:(num_year_columns + 1), part = "header", border = officer::fp_border(color = "black", width = 1)) |>
  autofit()

```

```{r group_bars}
#| echo: false

homeless_greater_than_change |>
  filter(
    academic_year == "2022-23",
    district_name == "Fairfield-Suisun Unified",
    indicator == "suspension"
  ) |> 
    mutate(students_to_orange = students_to_orange * -1) |> 
  # fct_reorder makes the chart look much cleaner by sorting bars by size
  ggplot(aes(y = fct_reorder(reporting_long, currnumer))) +
  
  # Base bar: Added alpha to make the solid colors a bit softer so outlines pop
  geom_col(aes(x = currnumer, fill = reporting_long), alpha = 0.5) +
  
  # Overlay 1: fill and color moved OUTSIDE aes() to make them literal
  geom_col(
    aes(x = homeless_student_enrollment), 
    fill = "transparent", 
    color = "blue", 
    linewidth = 1
  ) +
  
  # Overlay 2: Same trick here for the orange boxes
  geom_col(
    aes(x = students_to_orange), 
    fill = "transparent", 
    color = "darkorange", 
    linewidth = 1
  ) +
  
  # Labels and text
  labs(
    title = "Suspension Status vs. Homeless Youth Overlap",
    subtitle = "Sample Solano District (2022-23)",
    x = "Number of Students",
    y = NULL,
    caption = "Solid Fill: Total suspended students in group.\nBlue Box: Students in this student group who are also in the Homeless Youth group.\nOrange Box: Reduction in suspensions needed to reach the Orange tier."
  ) +
  
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    # Disable the legend globally
    legend.position = "none",
    
    # Text sizing and formatting
    plot.title = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold"),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 12, face = "italic", margin = margin(b = 10)),
    plot.caption = element_text(size = 10, hjust = 0, color = "gray30", margin = margin(t = 15)),
    
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 11, color = "black"),
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 11),
    axis.title.x = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold", margin = margin(t = 10)),
    
    # Remove horizontal grid lines for a cleaner bar chart appearance
    panel.grid.major.y = element_blank()
  )

```

```{r group_bars_2}
#| echo: false

homeless_greater_than_change |>
  filter(
    academic_year == "2022-23",
    district_name == "Fairfield-Suisun Unified",
    indicator == "suspension"
  ) |> 
  mutate(students_to_orange = students_to_orange * -1) |> 
  ggplot(aes(y = fct_reorder(reporting_long, currnumer))) +
  
  # Base bar: Anchored at 0, ends at currnumer
  geom_col(aes(x = currnumer, fill = reporting_long), alpha = 0.5) +
  
  # Overlay 1: Blue Box
  geom_tile(
    aes(
      x = currnumer - (students_to_orange + (0.01 * homeless_student_enrollment)), 
      width = homeless_student_enrollment
    ), 
    height = 0.9, # 0.9 perfectly matches standard geom_col thickness
    fill = "transparent", 
    color = "blue", 
    linewidth = 1
  ) +
  
  # Overlay 2: Orange Box flush to the right edge of the base bar
  geom_tile(
    aes(
      x = currnumer - (students_to_orange / 2), 
      width = students_to_orange
    ), 
    height = 0.9, 
    fill = "transparent", 
    color = "darkorange", 
    linewidth = 1
  ) +
  
  # Labels and text
  labs(
    title = "Suspension Status vs. Homeless Youth Overlap",
    subtitle = "Sample Solano District (2022-23)",
    x = "Number of Students",
    y = NULL,
    caption = "Solid Fill: Total suspended students in group.\nBlue Box: Students in this group who are also in the Homeless Youth group.\nOrange Box: Reduction in suspensions needed to reach the Orange tier."
  ) +
  
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    plot.title = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold"),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 12, face = "italic", margin = margin(b = 10)),
    plot.caption = element_text(size = 10, hjust = 0, color = "gray30", margin = margin(t = 15)),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 11, color = "black"),
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 11),
    axis.title.x = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold", margin = margin(t = 10)),
    panel.grid.major.y = element_blank()
  )

```

```{r group_bars_3}
#| echo: false

homeless_greater_than_change |>
  filter(
    academic_year == "2022-23",
    district_name == "Fairfield-Suisun Unified",
    indicator == "suspension"
  ) |> 
  mutate(students_to_orange = students_to_orange * -1) |> 
  ggplot(aes(y = fct_reorder(reporting_long, currnumer))) +
  
  # NEW: Background bar showing total students (currdenom)
  # Plotted first so it sits behind everything else
  geom_col(aes(x = currdenom), fill = "gray90", width = 0.9) +
  
  # Base bar: Anchored at 0, ends at currnumer (Suspended students)
  geom_col(aes(x = currnumer, fill = reporting_long), alpha = 0.6, width = 0.9) +
  
  # Overlay 1: Blue Box flush to the right edge of the currnumer bar
  geom_tile(
    aes(
      x = currnumer - (homeless_student_enrollment/8), 
      width = homeless_student_enrollment
    ), 
    height = 0.9, 
    fill = "transparent", 
    color = "blue", 
    linewidth = 1
  ) +
  
  # Overlay 2: Orange Box flush to the right edge of the currnumer bar
  geom_tile(
    aes(
      x = currnumer - (students_to_orange / 2), 
      width = students_to_orange
    ), 
    height = 0.9, 
    fill = "transparent", 
    color = "darkorange", 
    linewidth = 1
  ) +
  
  # Labels and text
  labs(
    title = "Suspension Status vs. Homeless Youth Overlap",
    subtitle = "Sample Solano District (2022-23)",
    x = "Number of Students",
    y = NULL,
    caption = "Gray Background: Total students in group.\nColored Fill: Total suspended students in group.\nBlue Box: Students in this group who are also in the Homeless Youth group.\nOrange Box: Reduction in suspensions needed to reach the Orange tier."
  ) +
  
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    plot.title = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold"),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 12, face = "italic", margin = margin(b = 10)),
    plot.caption = element_text(size = 10, hjust = 0, color = "gray30", margin = margin(t = 15)),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 11, color = "black"),
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 11),
    axis.title.x = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold", margin = margin(t = 10)),
    panel.grid.major.y = element_blank()
  )

```
